raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The average Golden State family spent over $3,000 out-of-pocket on their healthcare in 2018.",
    "Out-of-pocket health costs can prevent California's youth from accessing necessary healthcare.",
    "AB 298 would prohibit out-of-pocket health costs for youth in California.",
    "Removing out-of-pocket costs could lead to healthier children and adults who contribute to the economy and reduce strain on the health system.",
    "Early diagnosis and treatment are crucial for mitigating adverse health outcomes.",
    "Out-of-pocket costs have been found to reduce access to care, with 30% of families on high out-of-pocket plans more likely to delay seeking healthcare for their children.",
    "Delaying necessary medical care due to financial concerns can lead to a worsening of children's conditions."
  ]
}
```
